title: Parameter with data type of 'logic' gives syntax errors.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Copy the following code to a new file (For example "example.sv")
```
module example
  #(
    parameter integer               COUNTBITS = 8,
    parameter logic [COUNTBITS-1:0] MAXCOUNT  = 8'b1000000
  ) (
  input  wire        CLK, 
  output wire [15:0] LD
  );

  wire                clock;
  reg [15:0]          leds;
  reg [COUNTBITS-1:0] counter;

  assign LD    = leds;
  assign clock = CLK;

  always @(posedge clock) begin
    counter <= counter + 1;
    if (counter[COUNTBITS-1] == MAXCOUNT)
      leds <= 16'h8000 >> counter[COUNTBITS-2:COUNTBITS-5];
    else
      leds <= 16'h0001 << counter[COUNTBITS-2:COUNTBITS-5];
  end
endmodule
```

Inside yosys, run
```
yosys> read -sv example.sv
```

## Expected behavior

The file is loaded without errors

## Actual behavior

After the loading the file, the following error appears and yosys exits

```
yosys> read -sv example.sv
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: example.v
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `example.sv' to AST representation.
example.sv:4: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_LOGIC
```

## Comments

1) Removing the logic data type to the parameter "MAXCOUNT" fixes the issue
2) The Vivado compiler doesn't give any problems with the data type of the parameter
code_blocks:
yosys> read -sv example.sv
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: example.v
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `example.sv' to AST representation.
example.sv:4: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_LOGIC
